channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Great Lakes region faces more snow after weekend of harsh holiday weather
item.description: ORCHARD PARK, N.Y. (AP) — After U.S. travelers battled through harsh weekend weather to reach home following the Thanksgiving holiday, residents of the Great Lakes region face the prospect of more snow through the week, forecasters said.
item.dc:creator: CARA ANNA, CAROLYN THOMPSON and JONAH BRONSTEIN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Ted Bundy lawyer reveals what 'totally fascinates' him about Bryan Kohberger case
item.description: Ted Bundy's former lawyer says the cases against his client and University of Idaho student murders suspect Bryan Kohberger share the same key misstep.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: California bill would give public university admission priority to slaves' descendants
item.description: SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — A California lawmaker said he will introduce a bill Monday that would give admission priority to the descendants of slaves at the University of California and California State University, the state’s two large public university systems.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIE AUSTIN, TRÂN NGUYỄN and OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Supreme Court to hear arguments on flavored vape regulations imposed after youth vaping spike
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court will hear a vaping case on Monday, weighing federal regulators’ decisions blocking sweet vaping products after e-cigarette use spiked among kids.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Australian police arrest 13 people and seize a record 2.3 tons of cocaine from a fishing boat
item.description: WELLINGTON, New Zealand (AP) — Australian police seized a record 2.3 tons of cocaine and arrested 13 people in raids after the suspects' boat broke down off the coast of Queensland, authorities said Monday.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: First transgender attorney to argue before the Supreme Court, challenging health care ban for minors
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — When the Supreme Court this week wades into the contentious issue of transgender rights, the justices will hear from an attorney with knowledge that runs deep.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Biden displays AIDS Memorial Quilt at White House to observe World AIDS Day
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden on Sunday displayed the AIDS Memorial Quilt on the White House South Lawn for the first time in observance of World AIDS Day.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: UN halts aid shipments through Gaza's main crossing after looting. It blames the crisis on Israel
item.description: The U.N. agency for Palestinian refugees said Sunday it is halting aid deliveries through the main cargo crossing into the war-ravaged Gaza Strip because of the threat of armed gangs who have looted convoys. It blamed the breakdown of law and order in large part on Israeli policies.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, SAMY MAGDY and TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: FBI warns public of holiday-related scams targeting shoppers, donors
item.description: (KRON) -- Criminals are exploiting the holiday season to perpetrate scams against shoppers and donors, the Federal Bureau of Investigation San Francisco said, and people need to be vigilant. "Criminals don't take holidays off," FBI Special Agent in Charge Robert Tripp said in a press release.
item.dc:creator: Bay City News, KRON4

item.title: Biden pardons his son Hunter despite previous pledges not to
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden pardoned his son, Hunter, on Sunday night, sparing the younger Biden a possible prison sentence for federal felony gun and tax convictions and reversing his past promises not to use the extraordinary powers of the presidency for the benefit of his family.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Saturn's rings will 'disappear' next year: Here's why
item.description: Saturn is best-known for its rings. But, for a short time next year, you'll hardly be able to see them.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KTLA 5

item.title: Lake-effect snow blankets the Great Lakes as Thanksgiving travelers head home
item.description: ORCHARD PARK, N.Y. (AP) — Lake-effect snow blanketed swaths of the U.S. as Thanksgiving travelers journeyed home this holiday weekend, with more snow expected to fall over the Great Lakes region through the week.
item.dc:creator: CARA ANNA, CAROLYN THOMPSON and JONAH BRONSTEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Girl Scout saves dad days after learning CPR with her troop
item.description: Just days before Vada Carawan's father suffered a heart attack, the 11-year-old and her Girl Scout troop had learned CPR.
item.dc:creator: Deja Parker, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 3 teens identified as victims of violent Northern California Tesla Cybertruck crash
item.description: Hundreds gathered in the Northern California city of Piedmont Friday night for a vigil honoring the three young people who died in a Tesla Cybertruck crash the day before Thanksgiving.
item.dc:creator: Tor Smith, KTLA 5

item.title: What to know about the plastic pollution crisis as treaty talks conclude in South Korea
item.description: BUSAN, South Korea (AP) — The world’s nations will wrap up negotiating a treaty this weekend to address the global plastic pollution crisis.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER McDERMOTT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Motherless man's search for answers ends at popular beach town with gruesome discovery
item.description: For decades, Michigan resident Richard Hanchett was searching for his biological mother. The FBI later told him that his mother was Cape Cod's "Lady of the Dunes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lake Effect Snow Warning extended; Sunday closures and travel alerts
item.description: (WJET/WFXP) — After a break in the snow Saturday, snow-covered roads and cars are still a major issue for thousands across the region after more than two feet fell, and some areas even saw more than three feet as crews continue to work around the clock to clear the roads, with interstate restriction
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, NewsNation

item.title: Video: Colorado officer uses kid's bike during pursuit
item.description: A Lone Tree police officer went above and beyond to try and catch a theft suspect who fled on a scooter in a neighborhood.
item.dc:creator: Maddie Rhodes, KTLA 5

item.title: Cucumbers sold in Utah, 25 other states recalled over Salmonella concerns
item.description: A produce company based in Arizona is recalling whole cucumbers that were distributed in more than two dozen states and Canada due to Salmonella concerns.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, ABC 4

item.title: Lake-effect snow heading for New York state could disrupt travel and football
item.description: BUFFALO, N.Y. (AP) — Black Friday was giving way to a white weekend in parts of New York state, with the first big snow of the season threatening to bury towns along lakes Erie and Ontario during a hectic holiday travel and shopping weekend.
item.dc:creator: CAROLYN THOMPSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Amazon worker strike: 5 things to know
item.description: The strike, dubbed "Make Amazon Pay," has planned demonstrations in more than 20 countries including the United States, and the protests are slated to last through Cyber Monday.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation

item.title: First popularly elected Black mayor in New England, Thirman Milner, has died at 91
item.description: HARTFORD, Conn. (AP) — Former Hartford Mayor Thirman Milner, the first popularly elected Black mayor in New England, has died, the Connecticut NAACP said on Friday. He was 91.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: British lawmakers move closer to allowing terminally ill adults to end their lives
item.description: Members of Parliament approved the assisted dying bill by a 330-275 vote, signaling their approval in principle for the bill, which will undergo further scrutiny before it goes to a final vote.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Auto industry may lose 17 percent of profits due to Trump’s tariff plan: Report
item.description: Carmakers in the U.S. and Europe could lose up to 17 percent of their combined annual core profits in a worst-case scenario if the U.S. imposes steep tariffs on Europe, and two key U.S. allies, Mexico and Canada, a report from S&P Global released Friday showed.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Chicago Bears fire head coach Matt Eberflus after disastrous Thanksgiving loss
item.description: The Chicago Bears have fired head coach Matt Eberflus following a six-game losing streak, with offensive coordinator Thomas Brown serving as interim coach.
item.dc:creator: Marisa Rodriguez, KRON4

item.title: DNA test brings NY father, daughter together after nearly 50 years
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Thanksgiving is more than a tradition – it's a time for loved ones and families to get together, to embrace and give thanks for all that is good in their lives. For one family in Rockland County, this is a special holiday. A daughter and her biological father are together.
item.dc:creator: Marvin Scott, WGN 9

item.title: Democratic senators say they bungled border security in 2024
item.description: Democratic senators are privately acknowledging their party committed “political malpractice” by bungling the issue of border security, which they view as a driving factor behind President-elect Trump’s sweeping victory and their loss of four Senate seats.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Record Black Friday, holiday shopping expected despite inflation
item.description: A Black Friday shopping frenzy is expected once again this year despite financial constraints facing many across America.
item.dc:creator: Mills Hayes, NewsNation

item.title: Donald Trump's call for 'energy dominance' is likely to run into real-world limits
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is set to create a National Energy Council that he says will establish American “energy dominance” around the world as he seeks to boost U.S. oil and gas drilling and move away from President Joe Biden’s focus on climate change.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: JonBenét Ramsey: Boulder Police address case after Netflix series
item.description: Without specifically addressing the docuseries, Boulder Police called many of the claims in the show "completely false.
item.dc:creator: Maddie Rhodes, NewsNation

item.title: Macy’s Thanksgiving Day Parade: Big balloons, wet weather and 21 protesters arrested
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Macy's Thanksgiving Day Parade marched, soared and roared into its second century on Thursday despite a drenching rain and a brief disruption from pro-Palestinian demonstrators.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Amazon workers on strike from Black Friday to Cyber Monday
item.description: (WFXR) - Amazons workers across 20 countries, including the United States, are striking against what the organizing labor union calls anti-worker and anti-democratic practices.
item.dc:creator: Sydney Jaxtheimer, Fox 5

item.title: Why retailers still see Black Friday as the high point of the holiday shopping season
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — After weeks of plugging increasingly inviting discounts, retailers in the United States and several other countries are preparing for prime time: Black Friday, the bargain bonanza that still reigns as the unofficial kickoff of the holiday shopping season even if it's lost some luster
item.dc:creator: ANNE D'INNOCENZIO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Photos: 100 years of the Macy's Thanksgiving Day Parade
item.description: The parade in New York City, presented by department store chain Macy's, was first held in 1924 under the heading "Macy's Christmas Parade" to promote holiday sales and spotlight the newly expanded and, at the time, largest in the world Herald Square store in Manhattan.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Caldwell, Stacker, ABC 4

item.title: 2 injured after plane crashes into tree during emergency landing in California
item.description: Two people were injured Monday afternoon after a plane was forced to make an emergency landing and struck a tree.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, WFLA, NBC

item.title: How to avoid starting 'phantom traffic jams' this holiday travel season
item.description: Bumper-to-bumper traffic is a regular occurrence for holiday travelers. However, researchers with Vanderbilt University hope findings from a large traffic study can help.
item.dc:creator: Kori Johnson, ABC 4

item.title: Judge finds New York City in contempt over jail conditions, moves closer to a federal takeover
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A judge on Wednesday found New York City in contempt for failing to staunch violence and brutality at its jails, a scathing ruling that puts the troubled Rikers Island jail complex on the verge of a federal takeover. In a written decision, U.S.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, MICHAEL R. SISAK and JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4
